_purpose: A small set of 3D / 2.5D games authored MOBILE-FIRST: every goal demands on-screen touch controls (virtual joystick + drag-look + >=44px buttons), no pointer-lock, a compact bottom-anchored weapon/viewmodel (tjs-026/ray-014), and a coarse-pointer perf budget (tjs-027/mob-015). Run via clean_batch_driver.py AFTER relaunching the app so the new wisdom (three-js v5, raycast v2, mobile-web v2) is loaded. Genres chosen to actually feel good on a phone: runner/racer/tilt-maze/2.5D-road, plus one touch FPS to validate the small-viewmodel + on-screen-controls rules.

entries.goal: Build a 3D endless runner that is GREAT on mobile: sprint down a neon corridor, swipe left/right (and tap to jump) to dodge obstacles and grab coins, speed ramps up, score + distance HUD. Touch-first: large swipe zones + a jump button (>=44px); also support arrow keys on desktop. No pointer-lock. Bright, readable, 60fps with a lower obstacle budget on phones.

Build a 3D hover-racer playable on mobile: steer with an on-screen left thumb-stick (or device tilt) + an accelerate button on the right, 3 laps of a glowing canyon circuit with a lap timer. Touch-first, no keyboard required, no pointer-lock. Smooth delta-time motion, bright track edges, compact HUD that fits portrait and landscape.

Build a 3D marble tilt-maze for phones: TILT the device (and an on-screen joystick fallback) to roll a ball through a labyrinth to the goal, avoiding holes, with a timer and a restart button. Use device-orientation tilt as the primary control with a touch joystick fallback; no keyboard or pointer-lock needed. Bright top light, real rolling feel, portrait-friendly board.

Build an OutRun-style 2.5D pseudo-3D road racer that plays on mobile: hold the left/right halves of the screen (or on-screen arrows) to steer a scaling-sprite road that curves and hills at sunset, dodge traffic, speedo + timer. Touch-first, no keyboard required. Vibrant Miami-sunset palette, 60fps, layout works in portrait and landscape.

Build a 3D arena shooter that is FULLY playable on mobile touch: LEFT on-screen virtual joystick to move, RIGHT side drag-to-look, and a fire button (>=44px, bottom-right) -- never requestPointerLock. CRITICAL: the first-person weapon must be a COMPACT viewmodel anchored to the lower-right, occupying at most ~1/4 of the screen, so the arena and enemies stay fully visible (do not let the gun fill the view). Drone enemies, health/ammo/wave HUD, bright readable lighting, 60fps with a reduced budget on phones.
